title: synth_xilinx miscompilation: outputs are dropped if another output bit is driven 'Z'
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

With the following `repro.il`:

```
autoidx 1
module \repro
  wire width 2 output 1 \foo
  connect \foo 2'1z
end
```

Run `yosys -p 'read_rtlil repro.il; synth_xilinx; write_rtlil repro_synth.il'`

This produces a warning `Warning: Wire repro.$iopadmap$foo [1] is used but has no driver.`, which points to the problem - excerpt from `repro_synth.il`:

```
module \repro
  attribute \unused_bits "0"
  wire width 2 $iopadmap$foo
  wire width 2 output 1 \foo
  attribute \keep 1
  cell \OBUF $iopadmap$repro.foo
    connect \I $iopadmap$foo [1]
    connect \O \foo [1]
  end
  attribute \keep 1
  cell \OBUFT $iopadmap$repro.foo[0]
    connect \I 1'x
    connect \O \foo [0]
    connect \T 1'1
  end
end
```

`\foo[1]` is driven by `$iopadmap$foo[1]`, but `$iopadmap$foo` is never assigned anywhere.

```
$ yosys --version
Yosys 0.17+67 (git sha1 01cb02c81, gcc 12.1.0 -march=x86-64 -mtune=generic -O2 -fno-plt -fexceptions -fstack-clash-protection -fcf-protection -fvar-tracking-assignments -ffile-prefix-map=/build/yosys-git/src=/usr/src/debug -fPIC -Os)
```

## Expected behavior

Bit 0 of `\foo` outputs `Z`, bit 1 outputs `1`.

## Actual behavior

All non-`Z` bits of `\foo` are connected to a net with no driver.
code_blocks:
autoidx 1
module \repro
  wire width 2 output 1 \foo
  connect \foo 2'1z
end

module \repro
  attribute \unused_bits "0"
  wire width 2 $iopadmap$foo
  wire width 2 output 1 \foo
  attribute \keep 1
  cell \OBUF $iopadmap$repro.foo
    connect \I $iopadmap$foo [1]
    connect \O \foo [1]
  end
  attribute \keep 1
  cell \OBUFT $iopadmap$repro.foo[0]
    connect \I 1'x
    connect \O \foo [0]
    connect \T 1'1
  end
end
